summarization.a: The chart shows the change in CoOx thickness over cycles at two temperatures, 200 °C and 250 °C. The rate of change are 0.09 Å/cycle and 0.43 Å/cycle for 200 °C and 250 °C, respectively.

a.question: Which parameters are being evaluated in this figure?
a.answer: The change in the CoOx thickness is evaluated as a function of the temperature and the number of cycles.

a.question: How does the CoOx thickness change with the number of cycles?
a.answer: The thickness of the CoOx decreases as the number of cycles increases for both temperatures, 200 °C and 250 °C.

a.question: Which temperature results in greater changes to the CoOx thickness?
a.answer: As can be seen in the figure, a higher temperature (i. e. 250 °C) produces greater changes in the CoOx thickness, reaching an etch per cycle value of 0.43 Å/cycle.

a.question: What performance advantage at 250 °C?
a.answer: At this temperature, it is possible to achieve a higher etch rate of 0.43 Å/cycle compared to 0.09 Å/cycle at 200 °C.
